name: Fruit Stuffed Pork Chops with Apple Rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Toast the raisin bread crumbs in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden brown, stirring occasionally.
3. In a large bowl, mix together diced apple, pear, onion, celery, melted margarine, sugar, salt, pepper, and ground sage.
4. Place pork chops between two pieces of plastic wrap and pound to an even thickness of about 1/2 inch.
5. Spoon the fruit mixture into each pork chop, dividing evenly among them.
6. Wrap each pork chop in a piece of plastic wrap, securing with toothpicks if needed.
7. Heat oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
8. Sear pork chops on all sides until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
9. Reduce heat to low and add apple juice and water to the skillet.
10. Transfer skillet to preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes or until internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
11. Remove toothpicks from pork chops and serve immediately.
